items.title: People loved moving to Tasmania during the pandemic — but not anymore

children.content: From being a hot destination 10 years ago, Tasmania's population growth has ground to a halt — and big changes will be needed to reverse the trend, experts say.

gallery.altText: Family of three pose for photo on suburban street with their bikes

thumbnail.altText: Family of three pose for photo on suburban street with their bikes

items.title: New mission for volunteers who helped find Celine and Peter

children.content: A group of volunteers helped find the remains of two long-term missing people in Tasmania this year. Now, they're planning to look for Michael Vrankovic, who has not been seen since January.

image.altText: A group of people wearing high visibility clothing search in a line, walking through dry eucalypt forest in the mist.

items.title: Tasmania's police minister apologises for naming Port Arthur gunman

children.content: Felix Ellis has apologised for comments he made during budget estimates about the 1996 Port Arthur massacre in which he named the gunman, with one survivor labelling his comments "hurtful" and "disrespectful".

image.altText: A bearded man seen in profile.

items.title: Maths genius, devoted mum, headline-making CEO on King's Birthday Honours roll

children.content: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order.

gallery.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

items.title: The barrel-makers at the heart of Tasmania's whisky industry

children.content: Master cooper Dave Schmeider spent nearly 56 years in the barrel-making trade and reckons his "nose is stuffed" and his body is telling him to "back up"; luckily, his daughter, Mell, has followed him into the business.

image.altText: a woman sets a whisky barrel on fire and hoses it down

items.title: Paramedics warn of burnout and trauma as ambulance wait times exceed 'worst case scenario'

children.content: Average wait times for ambulances in Tasmania have exceeded worst-case scenario projections, despite extra resourcing, with many staff wanting to change careers, new survey results show.

image.altText: Ambulance Tasmania paramedic at rear of vehicle.

items.title: How Tasmania's planned new stadium could trigger deal for Hobart Hurricanes

children.content: Cricket's smallest state says it could become a major player in privatisation, with a potential Hobart Hurricanes deal tied to Tasmania's planned Macquarie Point roofed stadium.

gallery.altText: A group of Hobart Hurricanes cricketers stand on a podium and celebrates while holding a trophy as confetti falls around them.
An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.

thumbnail.altText: A group of Hobart Hurricanes cricketers stand on a podium and celebrates while holding a trophy as confetti falls around them.
An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.

items.title: When a detective told a witness Joanna was pregnant, she took police to VCAT

children.content: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

gallery.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

thumbnail.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

items.title: From migration to AUKUS, more politicians are speaking out

children.content: Former Coalition frontbencher Paul Scarr says inflammatory migration rhetoric is taking a toll on migrant communities. His warning comes as both major parties wrestle with populist pressures.

image.altText: Man standing in a suit in the senate

items.title: Quick hits: Monaco GP interrupted for safety as Kardashians keep up with F1

children.content: From a dodgy section of track to a mega celebrity watching her partner equal a record with the great Ayrton Senna, the 2026 Monaco Grand Prix had plenty of talking points.

image.altText: Kim Kardashian looks on in parc ferme at Monaco

items.title: Piastri promoted after penalty as teen sensation wins wild Monaco GP

children.content: Teenage sensation Kimi Antonelli was flawless to lead every lap of a drama-filled Monaco Grand Prix, claiming a fifth consecutive win to extend his lead in the Formula 1 drivers' championship. Follow live.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri at the Monaco Grand Prix 2026

items.title: Players left in tears as Danish star Eriksen collapses during friendly

children.content: Danish player Christian Eriksen collapses on the pitch in a match against Ukraine but is conscious, the Danish Football Association says in a social media post.

image.altText: Danish players show their distress after Christian Eriksen's collapse.

items.title: Why sentiment in Japan has turned against foreigners despite a worker shortage

children.content: Japan's answer to One Nation skyrockets in popularity after adopting an anti-immigration stance, despite Japan needing foreign workers more than ever.

image.altText: Sohei Kamiya, holding two microphones tape together, speaking with a closed fist in the air.

items.title: As rape allegations engulf MAFS UK, Channel 4's former boss says the risks were always there

children.content: Married at First Sight UK could be axed as its broadcaster, Channel 4, awaits an external review into the program and serious rape allegations before "deciding what happens next".

image.altText: A building with people walking past outside. There is a large '4' sign outside the building.

items.title: Shrek, Captain Cook and the stars: Welcome to Brian Robinson's multiverse

children.content: Newcastle Art Gallery presents the distinctive and intricate work of the Torres Strait Islander artist in its first major solo survey following a four-year refurbishment.

gallery.altText: A Torres Strait Islander man with a long grey beard and an earring in his right ear, wearing a black jacket.

thumbnail.altText: A Torres Strait Islander man with a long grey beard and an earring in his right ear, wearing a black jacket.

items.title: Famous outback race claims another life after serious motorbike crash

children.content: A motorbike rider has died in the Central Australian outback after a Finke Desert Race crash, with the two-day event expected to continue as police investigate.

image.altText: A row of motorbikes at the starting line, their tyres are kicking up clumps of the dirt track.

items.title: Six filmmakers had 10 days to make a movie overseas. See how they went

children.content: From illegal fishing in the Philippines to Michael Jackson impersonators in Las Vegas, catch up on the films from the very first week of Race Around The World.

image.altText: A fisherman sits on the end of his boat

items.title: Inside a Trump-linked plan to turn a communist-era military base into a luxe resort

children.content: Ivanka Trump has floated "manifestations" of luxury holidays. Meanwhile, Albanians are claiming "irreversible" ecological damage. Why protests are shaking Albania.

gallery.altText: A excavator is seen among sand.
Concept art of rooms jutting out of a hill.

thumbnail.altText: A excavator is seen among sand.
Concept art of rooms jutting out of a hill.

items.title: History quiz: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?

children.content: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

image.altText: A black and white image of a robot arm surveying the rocky, barren surface of Mars.

items.title: Daniel Turpin named as diver killed in WA shark attack

children.content: The family of a 35-year-old diver who died after being bitten by a shark has described him as "an adored husband, son, brother and uncle who had a lifelong love and deep respect for the ocean".

image.altText: A man with short brown hair and a white shirt.

items.title: Pauline Hanson tells Ben Roberts-Smith supporters she's 'walked in his shoes'

children.content: Pauline Hanson says, like Ben Roberts-Smith, she knows what it is like to face a criminal trial.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson speaking into media mics

items.title: 'Embarrassing' Tigers humiliated by Penrith, Sharks down Dragons

children.content: The Penrith Panthers thrash Wests 68-0 to record the biggest win in club history, as Cronulla notches a 34-12 victory over St George Illawarra at Shark Park.

image.altText: An NRL player in white and black and orange has his hand over his mouth in afternoon sun

items.title: 'Happening in every university': Students sent to Putin's 'meat grinder'

children.content: Lessons look a little different at some universities and technical colleges around Russia this year, as the Kremlin tries to entice students to enlist in the military to cover massive casualties in Ukraine.

A man in a suit looking on.
Cars drive near a billboard which has a picture of solders on it.

items.title: Blues beat Dons to make it four in a row, Swans pip Saints in a thriller

children.content: Carlton continues its resurgence under interim coach Josh Fraser with a five-point victory over Essendon, after Sydney completes a thrilling victory over St Kilda.

image.altText: An AFL player in dark blue celebrates a goal, framed by players in red and black

items.title: SA Police investigating after man dies after 'ineffective' taser arrest

children.content: Police are investigating an incident at Clare, in South Australia's mid north, where a man became unresponsive during an arrest.

image.altText: multiple officers on the street

items.title: Missing Melbourne teacher allegedly murdered by brother in India

children.content: Indian authorities allege Melbourne maths teacher Sunil Sharma was murdered by his brother and dumped in a canal. He had been in India to prepare a property for sale.

image.altText: Relatives of missing Melbourne man Sunil Sharma who are accused in relation to his disappearance and death

items.title: Hundreds in Nigeria freed from militant group Boko Haram

children.content: Hundreds of men, women and children abducted by militant group Boko Haram have been rescued, according to the Nigerian military.

image.altText: Men, woman and children gather on the ground at night under flood lights

items.title: How Beethoven's Ninth defined a genre, and the composers who got left out

children.content: Beethoven's Ninth has topped the ABC Classic 100 countdown for the fifth time. We explore its mystique and shed light on composers following in Beethoven's footsteps.

articles.title: ABC Classic 100: Greatest of All Time — by the numbers

children.content: Thousands of Australians voted in this year's ABC Classic 100. Here's what the numbers told us about your listening habits.

image.altText: A laptop and a violin on a desk

articles.title: Beethoven tops ABC Classic 100: Greatest Of All Time countdown — as it happened

children.content: After tens of thousands of votes in the 2026 ABC Classic 100 Ludwig van Beethoven has been crowned Greatest Of All Time. Look back at the full countdown.

items.title: Meet the man born, raised and married in his local town hall

children.content: Brian Baker, 95, spent the first 21 years of his life in living quarters at the York Town Hall where his father was caretaker.

image.altText: Man on mobility scooter dwarfed by large ornate stage and red velvet curtains

items.title: Commuters call for action after machete brawl at Flinders Street Station

children.content: Police say up to 10 people were fighting inside the station on Saturday night before some boarded a train still armed with machetes.

image.altText: A number of police officers stand on a platform beside a train with a person sitting on the ground near an officer.

items.title: Several infrastructure projects put on backburner as ACT 'at peak construction'

children.content: Some ACT government infrastructure projects, including health, emergency services and sports facilities in North Gungahlin, will be deferred to save about $700 million over four years.

image.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

items.title: Homelessness service concerned by changing demographic in clients

children.content: As cost of living and housing pressures intensify, a South Australian homelessness service says more young women and single mothers are reaching out for support.

image.altText: A person sleeping wrapped in a blanket in the middle of a grassy reserve, city streets and cars in the background

items.title: Debunking Australian weather's made-up terms and pseudoscience

children.content: Meteorological rhetoric in Australia, including from the media, can at times be inaccurate, misconstrued or just completely fictitious.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holds an umbrella in rain

items.title: Mourners farewell gangster a day after gunman targets wrong funeral venue

children.content: Dozens of mourners have turned out to farewell Sydney crime boss Lorenzo Lemalu at a funeral held at Lakemba Mosque, in Sydney's western suburbs.

image.altText: A crowd of people dressed in black clothing surrounding a black and white funeral arbour and black hearse next to a lawn.

items.title: As vaccination rates fall in Australia, a British mother has a warning

children.content: Measles killed Rebecca Archer's daughter Renae slowly and silently over a decade. She has a message for the growing number of parents hesitant to have their children vaccinated.

image.altText: A woman with a serious expression holds a picture of a young girl who is smiling.
